name: S.E.H Kelly
address: 1 Cleve Workshops, Boundary Street, London E2 7JD
description: East London menswear maker. Every cloth is woven and every garment made in the British Isles — including British-sourced buttons and trims.
evidence_note: Sources all fabrics, buttons and trims from regional British mills/makers; made in British Isles.

name: United Overalls
description: Selvedge denim jeans cut and sewn in North London using denim woven by Hewitt Heritage — the UK's only denim mill. One of the only entirely English-made pairs of jeans.
evidence_note: UK-woven selvedge denim (Hewitt Heritage, Lancashire); sewn at Blackhorse Lane Ateliers, London.

name: Private White V.C.
description: Historic Manchester outerwear brand making in its own riverside factory, with around 90% of raw materials sourced within 50km — Yorkshire wools, Manchester cottons.
evidence_note: ~90% of raw materials within 50km of Manchester factory; British mills.

name: Community Clothing
description: Patrick Grant's Blackburn brand making wardrobe staples through a co-operative of British mills and factories — yarn spun in Manchester, jersey knitted in Leicester, cut and sewn in Blackburn.
evidence_note: UK mill/factory co-operative; fabric spun, knitted and woven in the UK.

name: Maquien
description: East Yorkshire country tailoring. Cloth sourced solely in Britain, with wools and cashmere woven in the UK and all garments made in England.
evidence_note: Cloths sourced solely in Britain; wools/cashmere woven in UK; made in England.

name: Maude & Fox
description: Dorset womenswear, hand-cut and sewn from 100% British tweed sourced in Yorkshire and Scotland.
evidence_note: 100% British tweed (Yorkshire/Scotland); each piece hand-made in Dorset.

name: Chapman Bags
description: Carlisle bag maker since 1984, handcrafting luggage from Lancashire canvas, Borders tweed and English leather.
evidence_note: Lancashire canvas, Borders tweed, English leather, British woven/dyed canvas.

name: Trakke
description: Glasgow workshop hand-making waxed-canvas bags from Halley Stevensons waxed cotton, milled in Dundee.
evidence_note: Waxed cotton from Halley Stevensons (Dundee); handmade in Glasgow. (Hardware e.g. zips imported.)

name: Carradice of Nelson
description: Lancashire cycling-luggage maker since 1932, hand-stitching waxed cotton duck bags using UK-sourced materials wherever possible.
evidence_note: Handcrafted in Nelson; sources as many materials as possible from UK suppliers. CONFIRM waxed cotton duck is UK-woven for full Gold.

name: Barnes & Moore
description: Birmingham leatherworker making belts and small goods from English oak-bark-tanned leather (England's last oak bark tannery) with British-cast brass.
evidence_note: Belts from English oak-bark tanned leather + British brass. NOTE: some smaller goods use imported veg-tan leather.

name: John Smedley
description: Derbyshire knitwear made at Lea Mills since 1784 — fine-gauge merino and Sea Island cotton knitted in England.
evidence_note: Knitted in England since 1784. (Raw fibre imported, as all UK knitwear; knitted/finished in UK.)

name: HebTroCo
description: Hebden Bridge 'Trouser Town' brand making trousers, jeans and workwear in West Yorkshire from quality natural fabrics.
evidence_note: Made in UK; uses 'the best fabrics and trims they can get' (mix of British and imported cloth). Some lines may qualify Gold.

name: Billingham
description: Family camera-, laptop- and travel-bag maker, hand-made in Cradley Heath, England since 1973.
evidence_note: Made in England, real leather + solid brass. Canvas is cotton/polyester and FibreNyte is 100% polyester — fabric not clearly UK-milled, hence Silver.

name: Campbell Cole
description: Minimal leather wallets, card holders and small goods, designed and made in England.
evidence_note: Made in England but from Italian vegetable-tanned leather — imported main material, hence Silver.

name: Carl Stuart Tailors
description: Ossett bespoke tailors since 1929, constructing made-to-measure suits and shirts entirely in their West Yorkshire workshop.
evidence_note: Construction wholly in UK; cloth chosen by client (British and imported, e.g. Italian) — hence Silver by default.

name: Knepp Wild Range Meat
address: Knepp Estate, Dial Post, West Sussex
description: Organic, slow-grown longhorn beef and wild-range meat from the Knepp rewilding estate, sold at the Wilding Kitchen & Shop.
evidence_note: Own organic rewilding estate produce.

name: Gazegill Organics
address: Lower Gazegill Farm, Dancer Lane, Rimington, Clitheroe BB7 4EE
description: Centuries-old organic farm near Clitheroe with a farm shop, butchery and dairy selling its own and other British organic produce.
evidence_note: Own organic farm; butchery, dairy, deli.

name: Farm2Fork
address: Dalditch Farm, Dalditch Lane, Budleigh Salterton EX9 7AS
description: Family-run organic Devon farm selling grass-fed beef and lamb and pasture-raised poultry, direct from a self-serve farm shop and online.
evidence_note: Own organic Devon farm; grass-fed/pasture-raised.

name: Eversfield Organic
description: Devon organic farm with shops in Tavistock and Totnes, supplying ethically reared British meat and produce. Now incorporates Coombe Farm Organic (acquired 2023).
evidence_note: Own organic Devon farm; consolidated with Coombe Farm Organic (Crewkerne, Somerset) in 2023.

name: Isle of Wight Tomatoes
description: Grower of tomatoes in the fertile Arreton Valley for over 30 years, selling direct under The Tomato Stall.
evidence_note: Own-grown produce.

name: Pipers Farm
description: Devon-based network of small British family farms supplying grass-fed, traditionally reared meat direct to your door.
evidence_note: Network of small British farms; grass-fed, native-breed meat.

name: Daylesford Organic
description: Large organic Cotswolds farm shop with its own market garden and creamery — predominantly British, with a range of specialist imported deli lines.
evidence_note: Own organic farm produce, but stocks specialist imports (wine, deli) — hence Silver.

name: Tricker's
description: Goodyear-welted boots and shoes, 100% made in Northampton by the Barltrop family since 1829.
evidence_note: Made in Northampton. Silver: shoe leather (calf) typically imported from European tanneries.

name: Crockett & Jones
description: Fine Goodyear-welted footwear made in Northampton since 1879, still at the Magee Street factory.
evidence_note: Made in Northampton since 1879. Silver: calf leather typically imported.

name: Joseph Cheaney & Sons
description: Goodyear-welted shoes hand-made in Desborough, Northamptonshire since 1886.
evidence_note: Made in Desborough. Silver: calf leather typically imported.

name: Loake
description: Family shoemaker since 1880, producing Goodyear-welted footwear in Kettering.
evidence_note: Core Goodyear-welted ranges made in Kettering. Silver: leather imported; some lines made overseas — confirm range.

name: Edward Green
description: Refined Goodyear-welted shoes hand-made in Northampton since 1890.
evidence_note: Made in Northampton. Silver: calf leather imported.

name: Johnstons of Elgin
description: Cashmere and fine woollens spun, woven and knitted in Scotland since 1797 — still on its original Elgin site.
evidence_note: Spun/woven/knitted in Scotland (Elgin & Hawick). Gold by UK-milled rule (fibre imported, as all cashmere).

name: Begg x Co
description: Luxury cashmere and lambswool scarves and accessories woven in Ayr, Scotland since 1866.
evidence_note: Woven in Scotland (Ayr/Hawick mills). Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: William Lockie
description: Premium cashmere and lambswool knitwear made in Hawick, the home of Scottish knitwear, since 1874.
evidence_note: Knitted in Hawick. Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: Corgi
description: Hand-finished socks and knitwear made in Ammanford, Wales since 1892.
evidence_note: Knitted in Wales. Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: Pantherella
description: Fine English socks knitted at a family-owned factory in Leicester.
evidence_note: Knitted in Leicester. Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: Peregrine
description: Knitwear and outerwear made in England since 1796, from a family business in Cumbria.
evidence_note: Knitted/made in England. Gold by UK-milled rule. Confirm exact site.

name: Mackintosh
description: The original bonded-cotton raincoats, hand-made in Cumbernauld, Scotland — each cut and glued by a single craftsman.
evidence_note: Hand-made in Scotland, but the bonded cotton is produced in Japan — imported fabric, hence Silver.

name: Hiut Denim Co.
description: Jeans made in Cardigan, Wales, reviving the town's denim-making heritage.
evidence_note: Cut and sewn in Wales; denim typically imported (e.g. Japan/Italy), hence Silver.

name: Chatsworth Farm Shop
description: Estate farm shop in the Peak District selling Chatsworth estate produce and Derbyshire suppliers, most within a 30-mile radius.
evidence_note: Estate + Derbyshire suppliers (mostly <30 miles).

name: Ludlow Food Centre
description: Oakly Park estate food centre where ~80% of food is from Shropshire and neighbouring counties, and half is made on site.
evidence_note: 80% from Shropshire & surrounding counties; half made on premises.

name: Balgove Larder
description: Farm shop, butchery and café on the Strathtyrum Estate just outside St Andrews, championing Fife and Scottish produce.
evidence_note: Own/local Scottish produce; butchery on site.

name: Fodder
description: Yorkshire Agricultural Society farm shop and café sourcing locally from North Yorkshire farmers and producers.
evidence_note: Locally sourced North Yorkshire suppliers; FARMA-listed.

name: Gloucester Services Farmshop
description: Westmorland-family motorway farm shop built on fresh, locally sourced Gloucestershire and South-West produce.
evidence_note: Local-sourcing USP; some range items may be wider — generally local British.

name: Rhug Estate Organic
address: A5 Rhug Estate, Corwen LL21 0EH, Wales
description: Award-winning organic estate in North Wales selling its own organic meat, plus a bistro, drive-thru and skincare.
evidence_note: Own organic estate produce.

name: Goodwood Home Farm Shop
description: Shop for one of Europe's few self-sustaining organic farms — award-winning meat, raw milk, cheese, beer and gin.
evidence_note: Self-sustaining organic Home Farm produce.

name: Riverford Organic
description: Employee-owned Devon organic farm and nationwide veg-box delivery, growing in Devon and beyond.
evidence_note: Organic and largely British, but imports out-of-season produce (e.g. citrus) — hence Silver.

name: Fox Brothers
description: One of the last working cloth mills weaving wool flannel and worsted entirely in Somerset since 1772; sells cloth and accessories via The Merchant Fox.
evidence_note: Cloth woven in England (Wellington). Gold: UK-milled fabric.

name: Lavenham
description: Quilted jackets cut, quilted and finished in one Suffolk factory using British-made wadding and fabric from UK mills such as Halley Stevensons.
evidence_note: Made in Suffolk; British wadding + UK-mill fabrics. Gold (confirm shell origin per range).

name: Christys' London
description: Heritage hatmaker; wool felt produced in Stockport and hats blocked and finished in Witney. Supplies many other names too.
evidence_note: Felt made in UK (Stockport), blocked in Witney. Gold: UK-made felt.

name: Ettinger
description: Royal Warrant leather goods maker working in English bridle hide — 'the most English of all our leathers' — hand-stitched in the UK.
evidence_note: Signature range uses English bridle leather (UK-tanned). Gold; note some ranges use imported leather.

name: Barbour
description: Iconic waxed-cotton jackets still made (and re-waxed) at Simonside, South Shields, though much of the wider range is made overseas.
evidence_note: Waxed jackets made in South Shields; many other products imported, so Silver brand-wide.

name: New Balance (Made in UK)
description: The 'Made in UK' trainer line manufactured at New Balance's Flimby factory in Cumbria since 1982.
evidence_note: Assembled in Flimby; materials (suede/mesh) imported, so Silver (footwear precedent).

name: Globe-Trotter
description: Suitcases handcrafted in Hertfordshire on Victorian machinery, with bodies of in-house vulcanised fibreboard.
evidence_note: Made in UK; fibreboard body UK-made but leather trim imported, so Silver under strict rule.

name: Tusting
description: Family maker-brand designing and making leather bags in its Lavendon workshop since 1875.
evidence_note: Made in UK; uses a mix of English and Italian leather, so Silver under strict rule.

name: Emma Willis
description: Bespoke and ready-to-wear shirts made at a Gloucester atelier.
evidence_note: Made in Gloucester; cloth typically Swiss/Italian cotton, so Silver.

name: Turnbull & Asser
description: Jermyn Street shirtmaker with its own shirt factory in Gloucester.
evidence_note: Made in Gloucester; cloth imported (Italian/Swiss), so Silver.

name: Keelham Farm Shop
description: Yorkshire farm shop and butchery sourcing from 250+ local Yorkshire producers and farmers.
evidence_note: Sources from 250+ Yorkshire producers; own butchery. Gold (British-focused).

name: Cannon Hall Farm
description: Working Barnsley farm famous for home-produced beef, pork and lamb with traditional butchery.
evidence_note: Own farm-produced meat.

name: Farmer Copleys
description: Award-winning Pontefract farm shop selling locally sourced, fully traceable Yorkshire food.
evidence_note: Locally sourced, traceable British food.

name: Hollis Mead Organic
description: Organic Dorset dairy farm selling its own milk and dairy plus local produce.
evidence_note: Own organic dairy produce.

name: Loch Arthur Creamery & Farm Shop
description: Organic Camphill community farm near Dumfries rearing its own animals and making artisan cheese, dairy and bakery.
evidence_note: Own organic farm; creamery, bakery, butchery.

name: Macknade
description: Long-established Kent food hall championing local growers alongside a wide fine-food range.
evidence_note: Strong local sourcing but large continental/imported fine-food range, so Silver.

name: Secretts
address: Hurst Farm, Chapel Lane, Milford GU8 5HU
description: Surrey farm shop and pick-your-own growing its own produce, alongside a broad gourmet range.
evidence_note: Grows own produce but wide gourmet/imported range, so Silver.

name: Jamieson's of Shetland
description: The only firm taking Shetland wool from fleece to finished knitwear entirely in the Shetland Isles, with its own spinning mill in Sandness.
evidence_note: Cleaned, dyed, spun and knitted in Shetland from Shetland wool. Gold (fully UK incl. British wool).

name: Melin Tregwynt
description: Family woollen mill on the Pembrokeshire coast weaving distinctive double-cloth Welsh blankets and accessories since 1912.
evidence_note: Woven in Wales; British (Yorkshire) yarn. Gold: UK-milled fabric.

name: ERIBÉ Knitwear
description: Scottish Borders knitwear house making contemporary takes on classic Fair Isle knitwear in Melrose since 1986.
evidence_note: Knitted in Scotland. Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: Sunspel
description: Maker of fine T-shirts and underwear at its Long Eaton factory since 1937 — the last UK factory producing luxury tees.
evidence_note: Made in Long Eaton; cotton fibre imported and fabric origin not clearly UK-knitted, so Silver. (Some 'Made in Britain' lines.)

name: Drake's
description: Menswear and accessories maker; ties handmade in East London and shirts made at its own factory in Chard, Somerset.
evidence_note: Made in England; silk/cloth imported (e.g. Italian), so Silver.

name: NPS Solovair
description: Goodyear-welted boots and shoes made in Wollaston since 1881 — the original factory behind the first Dr. Martens.
evidence_note: Made in Wollaston; leather imported, so Silver (footwear precedent).

name: Dr. Martens (Made in England)
description: The 'Made in England' line still produced at the original Cobbs Lane factory in Wollaston (the wider brand is made overseas).
evidence_note: MIE line made in Wollaston; leather imported, so Silver (and brand largely imported).

name: Low Sizergh Barn
description: Lake District farm shop on a working dairy farm — award-winning cheese made with the farm's own milk and veg grown on site.
evidence_note: Own farm milk/cheese + on-site veg.

name: Trevaskis Farm
description: Family Cornwall farm with shop, butchery, pick-your-own fields and restaurant, selling its own and local produce.
evidence_note: Own farm produce + PYO + butchery.

name: Lobbs Farm Shop
address: Heligan, St Ewe, near Mevagissey, Cornwall
description: Farm shop at the Lost Gardens of Heligan, supplied by the family's own three Cornish farms.
evidence_note: Supplied by own three farms.

name: Ardross Farm Shop
address: A917 between Elie and St Monans, Fife
description: Award-winning farm shop on the East Neuk of Fife coast selling the farm's own and local Scottish produce.
evidence_note: Own farm + local Scottish produce; FARMA-listed.

name: Brocksbushes Farm Shop
description: Family-run Northumberland farm shop with butchery, pick-your-own and local produce.
evidence_note: Local produce + own butchery/PYO.

name: The Hollies Farm Shop
description: Cheshire family farm shop since 1959 with 70+ local suppliers and a butchery.
evidence_note: 70+ local suppliers but explicitly complemented by food 'from other regions and abroad', so Silver.

name: Cobbs Farm Shop
description: Berkshire farm shop, kitchen and garden centre selling local and own produce alongside a wider range.
evidence_note: Local + own produce but broad general range — Silver pending produce-mix check.

name: Essington Farm
description: Working farm near Wolverhampton with a farm shop, pick-your-own and home-grown fruit and veg.
evidence_note: Own farm produce + PYO. Gold pending range check.

name: Abraham Moon & Sons
description: One of the last vertical woollen mills in the UK, taking raw wool to finished cloth, throws and accessories in Guiseley since 1837 (also Bronte by Moon).
evidence_note: Vertically woven in Yorkshire. Gold: UK-milled fabric.

name: Hawico
description: Luxury cashmere knitwear made in Hawick since 1874.
evidence_note: Knitted in Hawick. Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: Tanner Bates
description: Artisan leather bags, belts and small goods handmade in a Dartington workshop in South Devon.
evidence_note: Handmade in Devon, near the J&FJ Baker oak-bark tannery; uses English leather. Gold (confirm leather per range).

name: Carrier Company
description: Family maker of sustainable heritage workwear, bags and accessories, all handmade in Norfolk.
evidence_note: Made by hand in Norfolk; fabric origin not clearly UK, so Silver under strict rule.

name: Blackhorse Lane Ateliers
description: London's craft jean makers, hand-making selvedge and organic denim jeans in Walthamstow since 2016.
evidence_note: Made in London; denim mostly imported (Turkey/Japan/Italy), so Silver.

name: Norman Walsh
description: Hand-made sports and retro trainers, manufactured in Bolton since 1961 — Britain's only wholly UK-made sports footwear.
evidence_note: Made in Bolton; materials imported, so Silver (footwear precedent).

name: Gonalston Farm Shop
description: Nottinghamshire farm shop selling fresh produce and meat direct from the farm.
evidence_note: Fresh/local produce, meat direct from farm.

name: Denstone Hall Farm Shop
description: Family farm shop and café on the Staffordshire/Derbyshire border championing local produce.
evidence_note: Family-run farm shop; local produce focus.

name: Loch Leven's Larder
description: Farm shop and café at Channel Farm with views over Loch Leven, selling own and Scottish produce.
evidence_note: On working farm; Scottish produce focus.

name: Cowdray Farm Shop
description: Cowdray Estate farm shop in the South Downs with traditional butchery, deli, cheeses and fine food.
evidence_note: Estate butchery + local, but deli/fine-food range includes wider/continental lines, so Silver.

name: Garsons
description: Surrey pick-your-own farm and farm shop growing its own seasonal fruit and veg, with a large garden-centre range.
evidence_note: Grows own PYO produce but wide garden-centre/general range, so Silver.

name: Stansted Park Farm Shop
description: Estate farm shop stocking food from 100+ Sussex and Hampshire artisan producers, with a full butchery and deli.
evidence_note: Strong local sourcing but deli/charcuterie/cheese range, so Silver.

name: Emmett's of Peasenhall
description: The UK's oldest artisan ham and bacon producer (est. 1820), still curing on its original Suffolk premises, with a shop and café.
evidence_note: Own-cured British ham/bacon since 1820.

name: Maple Farm Kelsale
description: Small family organic Suffolk farm selling its own vegetables, flour, honey and eggs.
evidence_note: Own organic produce (veg, flour, honey, eggs).

name: North Sea Clothing
description: Chunky wool 'Submariner' and naval-style sweaters knitted in England, based on Ministry of Defence originals.
evidence_note: Knitted in England. Gold by UK-milled rule (confirm wool origin).

name: Genevieve Sweeney
description: Luxury British knitwear made by small family-run UK factories in the Scottish Borders, Derbyshire, Leicester and Oxfordshire.
evidence_note: Knitted in the UK by heritage manufacturers. Gold by UK-milled rule.

name: Henry Poole & Co
description: The founder of Savile Row (since 1806), making bespoke suits entirely on the Row.
evidence_note: Bespoke, cut and made in London; cloth is client's choice (British or imported), so Silver by default.

name: Anderson & Sheppard
description: Bespoke tailor established on Savile Row in 1906, known for the soft 'drape' cut, made in London.
evidence_note: Bespoke, made in London; cloth client's choice, so Silver by default.

name: Gaziano & Girling
description: High-end Goodyear-welted and bespoke shoes crafted at their factory in Kettering.
evidence_note: Made in Kettering; leather imported, so Silver (footwear precedent).

name: Llwynhelyg Farm Shop
description: Ceredigion farm shop selling its own home-grown potatoes and salad plus local Welsh produce and 80 Welsh cheeses.
evidence_note: Own-grown produce + local Welsh growers; renowned Welsh cheese range.

name: Knitsley Farm Shop
address: East Knitsley Grange Farm, Knitsley, Consett DH8 9EW
description: Family-run County Durham farm shop with butchery, deli and bakery selling locally sourced meats and homemade goods.
evidence_note: Own farm + locally sourced; butchery/deli/bakery.

name: Tablehurst Farm
address: London Road, Forest Row, East Sussex RH18 5DP
description: Biodynamic and organic community farm producing its own meat, poultry, veg, grain and raw milk; BBC Best Food Producer 2022.
evidence_note: Own biodynamic/organic produce.

name: Plumgarths Farm Shop
description: Family Cumbrian farm shop built around a traditional butchery counter showcasing locally sourced produce.
evidence_note: Local/Cumbrian produce; traditional butchery.

name: Otterton Mill
description: Historic working mill with a farm shop and artisan bakery beside the River Otter, selling its own bread and local Devon produce.
evidence_note: Own bakery + local Devon produce.

name: Weetons
description: Harrogate food hall showcasing local Yorkshire produce alongside a home and garden centre.
evidence_note: Strong local sourcing but food-hall/garden-centre range, so Silver.

name: Emma Bridgewater
address: Emma Bridgewater Factory, Lichfield Street, Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent ST1 3EJ
description: Britain's best-known spongeware pottery. Every piece of cream earthenware is made start-to-finish at the Hanley factory, decorated by hand.
evidence_note: Clay dug in Devon, Cornwall, Wales and Staffordshire, blended by two Potteries-based suppliers; all making at the Hanley factory (company factory pages).

name: Denby Pottery
address: Denby Pottery Village, Derby Road, Denby, Ripley DE5 8NX
description: Stoneware made on the same Derbyshire site since 1809, using clay from the seam the pottery was founded on.
evidence_note: Derbyshire clay still mined within five miles of the factory; all production on-site at Denby (company history pages).

name: Burleigh (Middleport Pottery)
address: Middleport Pottery, Port Street, Burslem, Stoke-on-Trent ST6 3PE
description: The last Victorian pottery still making earthenware the original way — including the world's only surviving tissue-transfer printing.
evidence_note: Body from Devon ball clay and Cornish china clay; all ware made at Middleport since 1889 (company FAQ/about pages).

name: Moorcroft
address: W. Moorcroft Ltd, Sandbach Road, Cobridge, Stoke-on-Trent ST6 2DQ
description: Legendary art pottery famous for tube-lined florals, rescued in 2025 by the founder's grandson and making again in Stoke.
evidence_note: All making in Stoke-on-Trent (resumed 2025 under W. Moorcroft Ltd); clay body origin not publicly evidenced — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Leach Pottery
address: Higher Stennack, St Ives, Cornwall TR26 2HE
description: The birthplace of British studio pottery. Standard Ware is still thrown and reduction-fired in St Ives.
evidence_note: Stoneware clay from Doble's claypit, St Agnes, Cornwall; thrown and fired at the St Ives pottery (pottery's own product pages).

name: Whichford Pottery
description: Family pottery hand-throwing frostproof terracotta flowerpots since 1976, with a 10-year frostproof guarantee.
evidence_note: Refines its own clay from a blend of two British clays (Blockley and Ironbridge); all pots handmade in Warwickshire (company pages).

name: Highland Stoneware
description: Hand-made, freehand-painted stoneware from the far north-west Highlands since 1974; every piece signed by its painter.
evidence_note: Own clay, glaze and colour recipes, all mixed in Lochinver; made and decorated in Lochinver and Ullapool (company pages).

name: Royal Crown Derby
description: Fine bone china made in Derby in a line reaching back to 1750 — tableware, giftware and the famous paperweights.
evidence_note: All manufacture at the Osmaston Road factory, Derby; bone-china body ingredient sourcing not publicly evidenced — Silver pending confirmation.

name: 1882 Ltd
description: Design-led ceramics by the fifth generation of the Johnson Brothers family, working with leading designers, all made in Stoke.
evidence_note: Produced in Stoke-on-Trent across five clay bodies incl. bone china (company about page); body sourcing not publicly evidenced — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Portmeirion
description: Home of Botanic Garden since 1972. Core Botanic Garden plates, bowls and mugs are made at the Stoke factory.
evidence_note: Only sets explicitly marked 'Made in England' are made in Stoke; other pieces and ranges made overseas — Silver on the farm-shop analogy (UK maker, partial imported range).

name: Emma Alington
description: Luxury hand-crafted stoneware tableware, made in a Berkshire garden studio with some ranges made with craftsmen in Stoke.
evidence_note: All making in UK (Berkshire studio + Stoke craftsmen); 'black stoneware clay' origin not stated — Silver pending clay-source confirmation.

name: Feldspar
description: Fine bone china 'objects for life', slip-cast in Devon and Stoke and hand-painted at the family's Dartmoor studio.
evidence_note: Bone china body contains Cornish china clay (company pages); all making split between own Devon studio and Stoke — fully UK made and UK-material body.

name: Rye Pottery
description: Third-generation family pottery hand-making tin-glazed ware in Rye since 1947 — each piece passes through 12+ hand processes.
evidence_note: Everything made by hand in Rye; current clay-body source not publicly evidenced — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Dunoon Ceramics
address: St Mary's Works, Newcastle Street, Stone ST15 8LB
description: Family firm hand-casting the lightest fine bone china mugs in Britain at its Stone factory since 1982.
evidence_note: Company states bone-china clay from Cornwall and all making at the Stone, Staffordshire factory by slip hand-casting.

name: Linda Bloomfield
description: Hand-thrown porcelain tableware with satin glazes she mixes herself; stocked by Liberty and Michelin-starred kitchens.
evidence_note: Thrown in English porcelain in her west London studio; glazes mixed in-house (maker/Homo Faber profiles) — UK-prepared body.

name: Sue Pryke
description: Slip-cast porcelain tableware from a rural Leicestershire studio, by the Great Pottery Throw Down judge.
evidence_note: Handmade in Britain at her Leicestershire studio; porcelain body origin not stated — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Brickett Davda
description: Quietly beautiful handmade tableware in a palette drawn from British skies and sea, rolled, moulded and painted in Brighton.
evidence_note: Handmade in East Sussex studio (stockist and company pages); clay-body origin not stated — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Gwili Pottery
description: Welsh pottery hand-making and hand-painting its ware in the Gwili valley — home of the original Hug Mug.
evidence_note: Individually made and hand-painted at the Carmarthenshire pottery; clay-body origin not stated — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Edinbane Pottery
address: Edinbane, Portree, Isle of Skye IV51 9PW
description: Family pottery on Skye making wood-fired and salt-glazed stoneware by traditional methods since 1971.
evidence_note: All ware made at the Skye workshop; clay-body origin not stated — Silver pending confirmation.

name: Sytch Farm Studios
description: Restaurant-grade stoneware hand-thrown on a Shropshire farm, in white stoneware clay from the maker's home village pit in Cornwall.
evidence_note: Cornish stoneware clay sourced directly from the St Agnes clay pit; thrown, turned, glazed and fired at the Shropshire studio (company pages).

name: Belleek Pottery
address: 3 Main Street, Belleek, Co. Fermanagh BT93 3FY
description: Iconic parian china made on the banks of the Erne in Northern Ireland since 1857, using methods passed down for generations.
evidence_note: All making at the Belleek works, Co. Fermanagh; parian body mineral sourcing not publicly evidenced — Silver pending confirmation.
